violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as home fries) in the 2 door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated eggs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took unit out of service to be washed/sanitized. PIC disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Eggs and ham were observed held between 51-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC put eggs in cooler and ham was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink/dishwasher. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The floors in the back storage (with refrigeration) are constructed of carpeting which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Inspector observed sliced cheese with mold in 2 door cooler. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine and slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took units out of service to be washed/sanitized. PIC disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Home fries, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Pooled eggs were observed held at 55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed container in ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed containers of home fries cooling in 2 door unit, with lids, stacked on top of each other. PIC informed and discarded middle container due to time/temp. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the dishwasher/3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The floors in the back storage area (with refrigeration) are constructed of carpet which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (beverage station) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
Plumbing to the beverage station is in poor repair. Inspector observed a leak at pipes. System is to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
